series_metadata.series_description: When State Highway Patrol investigates a crime or incident, the Division will complete various forms for obtaining information during the response. At that time a case is established in the OTIS reporting system database. OSHP reports may contain the following documents: HP-24, HP-24F, HP-24K, HP-24L, HP-24R, HP-24S, HP-24T, HP-25, HP-25C, HP-25D, HP-28, HP-60, HP-70A, photographs/digital images, audio and/or video recordings, HP-12, HP-58A, BMV-3608, BMV 2431,HP-40B, etc.)

retention_rules.trigger_event: Microform: permanently.Referred in future and ongoing investigations. There is no specific ORC section requiring this retention, however these are OSHP case files which continue be useful public records requests, background investigations, historical purposes, and general documentation of criminal activity/statistics. OSHP often receive requests old reports from public, attorneys, universities, other governmental and non-profit agencies, and from our own investigators and Statistical Analysis Unit. ; Paper: on hard copy scanned and indexed into document storage system. Once hard copy has been scanned and indexed hardcopy can be destroyed. Investigation reports that involve capital crimes or are unique in nature shall also be retained in hard copy form permanently ; Electronic: permanently. Cases on microfilm must be scanned and indexed into document storage system before microfilm is destroyed.Referred in future and ongoing investigations. There is no specific ORC section requiring this retention, however these are OSHP case files which continue be useful public records requests, background investigations, historical purposes, and general documentation of criminal activity/statistics. OSHP often receive requests old reports from public, attorneys, universities, other governmental and non-profit agencies, and from our own investigators and Statistical Analysis Unit
retention_rules.disposition_method: Microform: Permanent ; Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Permanent
